reforms.description: Japan's government centralized the budget for science and technology, which had previously been managed by multiple ministries, to promote effective R&D and stimulate innovation through a coordinated approach.
reforms.component_name: Centralization of Science and Technology Budget
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Centralizing the budget is expected to enhance the efficiency of R&D funding and foster innovation by aligning resources with strategic priorities.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget for science and technology was centralized as part of the reform actions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how Japan manages its science and technology funding, which is crucial for enhancing its innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The budget for science and technology, which had been managed by a number of ministries, was centralised in the Council for Science, Technology and Innovation.

reforms.description: Japan implemented measures to create a 'venture ecosystem' aimed at fostering entrepreneurship and supporting the growth of start-ups through improved tax incentives and crowdfunding initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the entrepreneurial environment, these measures are expected to stimulate start-up creation and growth, contributing to economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: Measures to promote crowdfunding and improve tax incentives for business angels were launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: These measures represent a significant effort to enhance the start-up ecosystem, which is vital for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in Japan.
reforms.source_quote: The tax system for business angels was made more user-friendly and measures to promote crowd-funding were launched in 2014.

reforms.description: The government launched the Program on Open Innovation Platform with Enterprises, Research Institute and Academia (OPERA) in 2016 to strengthen linkages between academia, the business sector, and government research institutes, fostering university-industry partnerships.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between research institutions and businesses, leading to increased innovation and commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was launched to foster partnerships between academia and industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between sectors, which is crucial for driving innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2016, the government launched the Program on Open Innovation Platforms with Enterprises, Research Institutes and Academia (OPERA) to enhance university-industry partnerships to accelerate open innovation.

reforms.description: Japan is implementing reforms to promote entrepreneurship and firm creation, addressing regulatory barriers and improving the image of entrepreneurship to encourage more individuals to start businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging entrepreneurship can lead to increased innovation and productivity, creating new job opportunities and enhancing economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Reforms to promote entrepreneurship are underway as part of the 2016 strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic issues in entrepreneurship, aiming to significantly increase the number of startups and enhance the overall business environment.
reforms.source_quote: Policies to encourage firm creation will help create new opportunities for displaced workers.

reforms.description: Japan is upgrading its innovation framework to enhance R&D collaboration between universities and firms, aiming to reduce productivity gaps and foster innovation, particularly in SMEs and services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry can lead to increased innovation, productivity, and economic growth by leveraging research capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: Initiatives to encourage R&D collaboration are essential to raise productivity and equality.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to fundamentally change the landscape of R&D collaboration, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and productivity across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Initiatives to encourage R&D collaboration between universities and firms are thus essential to raise both productivity and equality.

reforms.description: Japan Finance Corporation is expanding the targets of loan programmes for start-ups in FY 2017, providing second opportunities for entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates access to financing for start-ups, promoting entrepreneurship and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The loan programmes for start-ups are being expanded.
reforms.importance_rationale: Important step to enhance support for start-ups, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Japan Finance Corporation is expanding the targets of loan programmes for start-ups in FY 2017, providing second opportunities for entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: The Cabinet Office Council for Science, Technology and Innovation formulated the National Energy and Environment Strategy for Technological Innovation towards 2050, focusing on promoting the innovation and diffusion of energy-saving and abatement technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance technological innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was formulated and is being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to align public investment with technological innovation goals, impacting both energy efficiency and environmental outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The Cabinet Office Council for Science, Technology and Innovation formulated the National Energy and Environment Strategy for Technological Innovation towards 2050.

reforms.description: The 5th Science and Technology Basic Plan in 2016 aims at achieving more effective use of government research grants by boosting the share of public research funds for universities that is allocated competitively.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing competitive funding, the reform is expected to enhance research quality and innovation output in universities, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was launched in 2016 with specific targets for funding allocation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public research funding is allocated, aiming to improve the effectiveness and competitiveness of research in Japan.
reforms.source_quote: The 5th Science and Technology Basic Plan in 2016 aims at achieving more effective use of government research grants.

reforms.description: Japan has launched the Japan Revitalization Strategy, which aims to raise firm exit and entry rates to enhance productivity by ensuring that non-viable firms do not hinder the growth of more productive firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the exit of non-viable firms allows for better resource allocation, which can lead to increased productivity and firm entry.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was set with specific targets for firm exit and entry rates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at improving productivity and resource allocation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The 2013 Japan Revitalization Strategy set a target of raising firm exit and entry rates from 4-5% to 10%.

reforms.description: The Guidelines for Personal Guarantees Provided by Business Owners were introduced to provide a common set of voluntary standards for self-regulation by SME groups and financial institution associations regarding guarantees by SME owners, aiming to expedite out-of-court settlements for debt resolution.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates smoother firm exit and restructuring, thereby promoting entrepreneurship and the entry of new firms, which can enhance productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Guidelines were introduced and have been in use since 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Guidelines represent a significant step towards improving the insolvency process for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering a more dynamic entrepreneurial environment.
reforms.source_quote: The Guidelines expedite out-of-court settlements for debt resolution within a framework of institutionalised procedures.

reforms.description: Japan has established the SME Training Institute, which offers seminars for owners and managers of SMEs, focusing on financial education to help them utilize market-based financing instruments beyond traditional bank lending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skills and knowledge of SME owners, facilitating better access to financing and promoting entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The SME Training Institute has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the SME Training Institute represents a significant effort to improve the entrepreneurial skills of SME owners, which is crucial for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Japan has established the SME Training Institute, which offers seminars for owners and managers of SMEs.

reforms.description: The government has launched educational programmes such as the Enhancing Development of Global Entrepreneur Program aimed at university and graduate students to foster entrepreneurial skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promotes the development of entrepreneurial skills among students, which is essential for increasing the number of entrepreneurs and enhancing innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Enhancing Development of Global Entrepreneur Program has been launched and is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets the education of future entrepreneurs, which is vital for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched educational programmes such as the Enhancing Development of Global Entrepreneur Program.

reforms.description: The government is planning to reform the credit guarantee system for SMEs, requiring banks to provide loans without guarantees and reducing the largest guarantee scheme from 100% to 80%.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to strengthen market forces by reducing reliance on government guarantees, encouraging banks to better assess credit risks and support viable SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to reform the guarantee system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the structural issues in SME financing, promoting a more sustainable and competitive environment for small businesses.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning to reform the guarantee system: i) banks applying for credit guarantees will have to supply loans to SMEs without credit guarantees.

reforms.description: Japan launched a four-year plan to digitalise the educational environment, investing 671 billion yen to equip schools with computers, electronic blackboards, and education software, aiming to enhance IT literacy among students.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in educational infrastructure enhances human capital and prepares students for a digital economy, which can drive future productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was launched with a specified budget and objectives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in educational infrastructure, which is crucial for fostering a digitally literate workforce.
reforms.source_quote: In 2014, Japan launched a four-year plan, totalling 671 billion yen (USD 5.9 billion) to equip schools with more computers...

reforms.description: The government aims to increase IT literacy across all age groups, particularly targeting older adults who have relatively poor IT skills, as part of its strategy to enhance productivity through digitalisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving IT literacy among the workforce enhances productivity and innovation capabilities, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government stated that it will consider policies to develop human resources to drive IT utilisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for enhancing skills, this program is part of a broader strategy and does not represent a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government stated that it will consider policies to develop human resources to drive IT utilisation at private-sector companies.
